sourceMovie.title: Puella Magi Madoka Magica the Movie Part I: Beginnings
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Puella Magi Madoka Magica the Movie Part I: Beginnings (2012) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spirited Away
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Puella Magi Madoka Magica the Movie Part I: Beginnings clicked for you, Spirited Away (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Puella Magi Madoka Magica the Movie Part I: Beginnings?
faq.answer: Try Spirited Away, The Boy and the Heron, Children Who Chase Lost Voices—each shares DNA with Puella Magi Madoka Magica the Movie Part I: Beginnings while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Puella Magi Madoka Magica the Movie Part I: Beginnings?
faq.answer: Puella Magi Madoka Magica the Movie Part I: Beginnings (2012) is best described as Thriller, Animation, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Puella Magi Madoka Magica the Movie Part I: Beginnings part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Puella Magi Madoka Magica: The Movie Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
